summary: Reference-quality coverage of the CS336 Assignment 3 (Scaling) deliverables, and a clear upgrade over the prior grade-3 audit: all five previously-flagged deficits are now fixed. Chapter 3.4 develops the Chinchilla parametric law L(N,D)=E+A N^-alpha+B D^-beta, derives the compute-optimal allocation via Lagrange multipliers (formula verified), and ships complete runnable code for all four deliverables: 6ND/FLOP accounting, sweep design under a constrained experimentation budget (new 'Designing the Sweep Under a Budget' section with a verified 24-run/1.32%-of-target table and a design checklist), BOTH the parametric fit (log-space Huber, now genuinely using scipy.special.logsumexp, multi-start L-BFGS-B, plus a bootstrap identifiability analysis) and the IsoFLOP parabola method (new runnable section recovering allocation exponent 0.447 vs analytic 0.452), and optimal-(N,D) projection with a fully worked 256-GPU/21-day budget example. Chapter 4.1 supplies the per-component FLOP breakdown (FFN 16d^2, QKVO 8d^2, scores 4sd = 24d^2+4sd/token/layer) via a dict-returning code function, covering the cost side of deliverable 1. I independently re-ran the deterministic optimal-allocation code and reproduced the book's exact claimed table (tok/param 32.1/50.1/78.2/122.1; losses 2.986->1.845) and the 42B worked-example figure. Verification (expected outputs, held-out extrapolation, bootstrap CIs), failure modes (Kaplan LR-schedule confound, under-converged runs, compensating-error identifiability with Epoch AI replication cited, data wall, emergence-is-metric-artifact), resource accounting, library pointers (scipy, torch.utils.flop_counter, DeepSpeed FLOPs profiler), and a 4-problem exercise set including a full-loop capstone are all present. A competent reader can build every deliverable from the text alone.
evidence: Verified by re-running: chinchilla_optimal reproduces the exact printed table (C=1e19 -> N=2.28e8, tok/param=32.1, L=2.986; ... C=1e25 -> 122.1, 1.845).
Verified: N=sqrt(C/120)=4.18e10 for C=2.1e23, matching the '~42B params / 840B tokens' worked example, and the throughput cross-check (460k tok/s over 21 days ~ 8.3e11 tokens) closes.
Optimal-allocation formula N_opt=[alphaA/(betaB)]^{1/(alpha+beta)}(C/6)^{beta/(alpha+beta)} derives correctly from d/dN of A N^-alpha + B(6/C)^beta N^beta; a+b=1 shown.

IsoFLOP section (prior deficit) is now fully coded: 5 fixed-C slices, np.polyfit parabola in log N, vertex -c1/2c2, recovers a~0.447 vs analytic beta/(alpha+beta)=0.452; grid-design guidance (4-6 slices, >=6 models bracketing both arms, center sqrt(C/120)).

Sweep-design section (prior deficit) now present: 4 slices x 6 models = 24 runs = 2.64e19 FLOPs = 1.32% of a 2e21 target, verified by construction, with per-run cosine-decay-matched-to-token-count, muP LR transfer, and a 7-point checklist.
Emergent-abilities bug fixed: smooth_per_token_accuracy now np.clip(...,0,1); logsumexp trick now actually used in predict_log_loss via scipy.special.logsumexp over stacked log-terms.

Exercises added: extrapolation-not-interpolation, reproduce-Kaplan-bias, loss-penalty-of-mis-sized-model, and a full-loop capstone (design sweep -> fit both methods -> confirm exponents agree within 0.03).

Roofline chapter gives per-token/per-layer FLOPs = 24 d^2 + 4 s d with correct sub-terms and a numeric 12x dominance check at d=4096,s=2048, plus transformer_flops_per_token returning a per-component dict.

deficits.missing: The 'contribution to LOSS' half of deliverable 1 is under-served relative to the cost half. The chapter states architecture (SwiGLU/RoPE/norm placement) shifts the offset A but gives no concrete component-ablation procedure (train with/without a component at fixed FLOPs, measure delta-loss, convert to an equivalent compute multiplier).
deficits.fix: Add a short subsection or exercise: hold C fixed, toggle one component (GLU vs vanilla MLP, RoPE vs learned pos, pre- vs post-norm), refit offset A, report the vertical loss shift as an equivalent compute multiplier. Cross-link to 02-transformer/06-transformer-block.

deficits.missing: CS336 A3's defining framing -- a budgeted black-box training API that returns loss for a submitted config, forcing the reader to spend a hard query budget and emit one optimal config for a larger target -- is not assembled into a single harness. All the pieces exist as separate code blocks but are not wired into the 'you have X FLOPs of queries, output one config, get scored' loop.
deficits.fix: Add a capstone lab wrapping true_loss as a budgeted query oracle: spend <=1-2% of the target budget on the IsoFLOP sweep, fit both methods, emit one (d_model, n_layers, D) prediction for target C, and score it against the oracle optimum -- turning the scattered blocks into the exact A3 deliverable.
